title: Purification of the any avian IgY using chloroform, trichloroacetic acid and affinity purification using HiTrap™ Columns.

steps: The IgY fraction of bantam hen was isolated by the chloroform-Trichloroacetic acid (TCA) -affinity chromatography method.
The egg is washed with warm water and the egg yolk is separated from the egg white.
The membrane is broken and the egg yolk collected.
The egg yolk is then diluted 1:3 in phosphate buffered saline (PBS), pH 7.4.
To 1/3 of the egg yolk mixture an equal volume of chloroform is added,
The mixture is then shaken and centrifuged for 30 min (1000×g, RT).
The supernatant is decanted and drop 1 ml of cold TCA and let the sample stand for 30 min at RT.
The mixture is then centrifuged as previously described.
The precipitate containing IgY is dissolved in PBS (pH 7.4) at a volume equivalent to 1/6 of the original volume of the egg yolk.
After that the preparation is dialyzed against 1L of PBS (pH: 7.4 for 24 h at 4°C).
The IgY is then removed from the dialysis tubing.
IgY concentration is assessed by an available methodology.
IgY samples then are further purified by affinity chromatography.
The preparation can be stored at –20°C.
By a sandwich ELISA the IgY concentration can be assessed.
Use Affinity Purification Using HiTrap™ Columns as follows:
Fill the syringe with distilled water.
Connect the column to the syringe
Snap off the tab on the column outlet.
Wash out the ethanol with distilled water.
Equilibrate the column with binding buffer.
The recommended ﬂow rate is 5 ml/min*.
Apply the sample using a syringe linked or ﬁtted to Luer connector
Use a ﬂow rate of 1 to 5 ml/min during sample application
Wash with at least 51 ml of binding buffer.
Maintain a ﬂow rate of 10 ml/min for washing.
Elute with 50 ml of elution buffer.
After elution, regenerate the column by using wash buffer
Re-equilibrate the column with 26 ml of binding buffer.
Store it until a new purification
